trm:term.trm:name: Authorization
trm:term.trm:definition: A preliminary approval confirming that the payer has sufficient funds and the transaction passes risk checks, without yet moving funds.

trm:term.trm:name: Merchant
trm:term.trm:definition: The party receiving payment in exchange for goods or services.

trm:term.trm:name: Acquirer
trm:term.trm:definition: The financial institution that processes payment transactions on behalf of the merchant.

trm:term.trm:name: Issuer
trm:term.trm:definition: The financial institution that issued the payment instrument (card, account) to the payer.

pr:section.pr:title: Authorization
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Verifying the payer can pay before moving funds.
pr:section.pr:p: When a merchant initiates a charge, the request flows through the acquirer to the issuer. The issuer checks:
The issuer responds with an authorization code (approved) or a decline reason.
pr:section.pr:note: Authorization places a hold on funds but does not transfer them. The hold expires if not captured within the network-defined window (typically 7 days for card payments).

org:task.org:purpose: The first phase of processing any payment.

rel:relation.rel:note: Authorization must complete before capture can begin.

art:mapping.art:note: Implements fund check and risk scoring. Does not yet implement geographic or velocity checks.
